item.title: What the Supreme Court did on the final day of its term
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the right of children born on U.S. soil to automatic American citizenship. In so doing, the court rejected President Trump's most aggressive attempt to limit immigration.

item.title: Editor's note: NPR retracts Justice Samuel Alito story
Serena Williams loses singles match in opening round at Wimbledon
item.description: Williams played two doubles matches just before Wimbledon to announce her comeback, but she hadn't played a singles match since the 2022 U.S. Open.

item.title: US women Olympians react to historic Supreme Court ruling on the protection of women's sports
item.description: Olympic gold medalists celebrate the Supreme Court ruling upholding state laws that ban transgender athletes from competing in women's sports.

item.title: Law professor discusses Supreme Court's final rulings this term
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Kim Wehle {WAIL-ee}, professor of law at the University of Baltimore School of Law, about the Supreme Court's final decisions this term.

item.title: NPR reporter explains retracted story on Alito's retirement
item.description: NPR journalist Nina Totenberg on Tuesday discussed the events leading up to a story from the outlet about Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito retiring that was later retracted. NPR said in an editor’s

item.title: White House upends AI model release
item.description: {beacon} View Online Technology Technology   The Big Story White House halts new AI models, spurring confusion The Trump administration's request for OpenAI to delay the release of its new model

item.title: Right-backs Quansah and James out of DR Congo game
item.description: Right-backs Jarell Quansah and Reece James will miss England's World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo because of injury.

item.title: Anthropic says US lifts export ban on its advanced AI tools
item.description: Fable and Mythos were abruptly suspended in June over concerns that they could be used by hackers.

item.title: Mbappe, Deschamps break World Cup records in Sweden thrashing
item.description: With his fifth and sixth goals at the tournament, Mbappe pulls even with Messi in the Golden Boot race.

item.title: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
item.description: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.

item.title: North Korea’s Kim hails ‘unshakeable will’ to develop ties with China’s Xi
item.description: Kim Jong Un has sent Xi Jinping a congratulatory message to mark the 105th anniversary of the Chinese Communist Party.

item.title: There's a Waterfall in Antarctica That Bleeds Red – Here's What's Hiding Inside It
item.description: Antarctica's Blood Falls are even weirder than they look. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s homegrown artists are knocking K-pop off its pedestal
item.description: Drawing lessons from the global success of K-Pop, the region is finding its own voice in a new generation of artists.

item.title: Algeria to vote in test of post-Hirak political landscape
item.description: Algeria holds legislative elections amid debates over reform, turnout and political stability.

item.title: LeBron James thanks LA Lakers ahead of free agency exit for 24th season
item.description: After eight years at the LA Lakers, James's next destination will be decided in NBA's imminent free agency period.

item.title: Tuchel will stick to Southgate's penalty plan
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel had revealed that his team will follow his predecessor Sir Gareth Southgate's penalty shootout blueprint at the World Cup.

item.title: Who is leading the race for the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot award?
item.description: Argentina's Messi is level on goals with France's Mbappe as World Cup favourites look to power through the knockouts.

item.title: Rutinel defeats Bird in Democratic primary for battleground Colorado House seat
item.description: State Rep. Manny Rutinel (D) is projected to defeat former state Rep. Shannon Bird (D) in the Democratic primary to take on Rep. Gabe Evans (R-Colo.), according to Decision Desk HQ. Evans, who represe

item.title: USA face Bosnia in World Cup knockouts, with pride, credentials on the line
item.description: Winning the whole tournament might be a stretch, but USA must look to regain momentum against Bosnia and Herzegovina.
